criteria: The agent has read Section II completely to understand the overall UNet-like architecture of UNetMamba consisting of ResT encoder, MSD decoder, and LSM.
The agent has read Section II.A completely to understand the ResT encoder with its four-stage design and Efficient Multi-Head Self-Attention (EMSA) mechanism.
The agent has read Section II.B completely to understand the Mamba Segmentation Decoder (MSD) and the VSS block structure including SS2D scanning.
The agent has read Section II.C completely to understand the Local Supervision Module (LSM) with its dual parallel convolution branches and train-only design.
The agent has read Section II.D completely to understand the combined loss function with principal loss and auxiliary loss components.
The agent has read Section III.A to understand the LoveDA and ISPRS Vaihingen dataset configurations including splits and image sizes.
The agent has read Section III.B to understand the experimental settings including optimizer, learning rate, epochs, and batch size.
The agent has read Table I to check quantitative results on LoveDA including per-category IoU and overall mIoU.
The agent has read Table II to check quantitative results on ISPRS Vaihingen including mF1, mIoU, OA, and efficiency metrics.
The agent has read Table III to check the ablation study results comparing the baseline, MSD-only, and MSD+LSM configurations.
The plan includes using a pre-trained ResT-Lite backbone as the encoder with four stages producing multi-scale feature maps.
The plan includes the Mamba Segmentation Decoder (MSD) incorporating VSS blocks from VMamba on the decoding side at four stages.
The plan includes the SS2D module scanning feature maps in four different directions with S6 selective scan as the core of the VSS block.
The plan includes the Local Supervision Module (LSM) as a train-only CNN-based module with dual parallel convolution branches.
The plan includes applying LSM blocks at decoder stages 2, 3, and 4 and summing their outputs for the auxiliary segmentation result.
The plan includes the combined loss function with principal loss (Ldice + Lce) and auxiliary loss (Lce) weighted by alpha=0.4.
The plan includes the LoveDA dataset with 2522 training, 1669 validation, and 1796 test images at 1024x1024 without further cropping.
The plan includes the ISPRS Vaihingen dataset with the 5-fold repeated random split of 9 test, 2 val, and remaining training images, cropped to 1024x1024.
The plan includes the AdamW optimizer with learning rate 0.0006 and weight decay 0.00025 for 100 epochs with batch size 8.
The plan includes the ablation study comparing baseline (ResT-Lite + original decoder), MSD-only, and full UNetMamba (MSD+LSM).
The plan includes comparison against all seven SOTA baselines: BANet, MANet, DC-Swin, UNetFormer, E-PyramidMamba, CM-UNet, and RS3Mamba.
The plan includes efficiency evaluation using parameter count (M), memory footprint (MB), and FLOPs (G) alongside accuracy metrics.
The ResT-Lite backbone is integrated as the encoder with four stages outputting feature maps at H/4xW/4x64, H/8xW/8x128, H/16xW/16x256, and H/32xW/32x512.
The EMSA mechanism is implemented with instance normalization and 1x1 convolution applied to the scaled dot-product attention as in Equation 1.
The MSD decoder is implemented with VSS blocks operating at all four decoding stages, each using two VSS blocks in sequence.
The patch expansion (PatchExp) operation is implemented to upsample feature maps in the MSD decoder before processing with VSS blocks.
The linear embedding layer in the main flow of the VSS block is implemented as F' = LinearEmbed(LayerNorm(PatchExp(F))) as in Equation 2.
The 3x3 depth-wise convolution followed by SiLU activation is implemented in the VSS block main flow as F'' = SiLU(DWConv(F')) as in Equation 3.
The SS2D module scans F'' in four different directions using ScanExp for each direction v in {1,2,3,4} as in Equation 4.
The S6 selective scan space state model is applied independently to each of the four directional scans as in Equation 4.
The ScanMerge operation combines the four S6-processed directional scans into a single merged feature map F̄ as in Equation 4.
The bypass flow in the VSS block applies linear embedding followed by SiLU activation in parallel with the main SS2D flow.
The VSS block performs element-wise multiplication between the LayerNorm-processed main flow output and the bypass flow output.
The VSS block outputs the final feature FMSD through a linear layer with a residual connection to the block input.
The MSD decoder outputs segmentation logits through a 1x1 convolution head applied to the final decoded feature map.
The final segmentation output from MSD is upsampled to the original input image size (HxW).
The LSM block implements two parallel convolution branches with kernel sizes 3x3 and 1x1, each followed by BatchNorm and ReLU6 as in Equation 5.
The LSM merges the two parallel branches by element-wise addition: F̃' = F̃'_1 ⊕ F̃'_3.
The LSM applies a Dropout layer to the merged feature F̃' before the final 1x1 convolution.
The LSM applies a 1x1 convolution consistent with the MSD segmentation head, followed by upsampling to original image size HxW, producing F̃ as in Equation 6.
Three separate LSM blocks with different input channel dimensions are instantiated and attached to the decoder at stages 2, 3, and 4.
The outputs from the three LSM blocks (stages 2, 3, 4) are summed together to produce a single auxiliary segmentation prediction.
The LSM is a train-only module that is disabled during inference so it adds zero computational overhead at test time.
The dice loss Ldice is implemented with per-sample, per-class summation over one-hot encoded predictions and targets as in Equation 7.
The cross-entropy loss Lce is implemented with per-sample, per-class log-probability summation using one-hot encoded labels as in Equation 8.
The principal loss Lp is implemented as the sum of dice loss and cross-entropy loss: Lp = Ldice + Lce.
The auxiliary loss La is implemented as cross-entropy loss applied to the summed LSM output (auxiliary segmentation prediction).
The overall training loss is implemented as L = Lp + alpha * La with alpha=0.4 as in Equation 9.
The LoveDA dataset is loaded with the official split of 2522 training images, 1669 validation images, and 1796 test images.
The LoveDA images are used at their original 1024x1024 resolution without any further cropping.
The LoveDA dataset is configured with 7 land-cover categories: background, building, road, water, barren, forest, and agriculture.
The ISPRS Vaihingen dataset is configured with 6 land-cover categories: impervious surface, tree, building, low vegetation, car, and background.
The ISPRS Vaihingen 5-fold repeated random split is implemented: 9 images for test, 2 images for val, and the remaining for training in each fold.
The ISPRS Vaihingen images are cropped into patches of size 1024x1024 pixels for all five folds.
The AdamW optimizer is configured with a learning rate of 0.0006 and a weight decay of 0.00025.
The training configuration is set to 100 epochs with a batch size of 8 for both datasets.
The mIoU (mean intersection over union) metric is implemented for accuracy evaluation on both datasets.
The mF1 (mean F1-score) and OA (overall accuracy) metrics are implemented for the ISPRS Vaihingen evaluation.
The efficiency evaluation computes model parameter count (Param in M), memory footprint (Memo in MB), and FLOPs (G) for a 1024x1024 input.
The code for the full UNetMamba model (ResT-Lite encoder + MSD decoder + LSM) has been executed successfully on the LoveDA dataset without errors.
The code for the full UNetMamba model has been executed successfully on the ISPRS Vaihingen dataset across all 5 folds without errors.
The ablation baseline model (ResT-Lite backbone with original RS3Mamba decoder, no MSD, no LSM) has been trained and evaluated without errors.
The MSD-only variant (ResT-Lite encoder + MSD decoder, without LSM) has been trained and evaluated on both datasets without errors.
The full UNetMamba ablation variant (MSD + LSM) has been trained and evaluated on both LoveDA and Vaihingen datasets without errors.
Inference on UNetMamba runs without the LSM module active (LSM disabled at inference time) and produces valid segmentation outputs.
The execution outcomes match Table I: UNetMamba achieves 53.35% mIoU on the LoveDA dataset, outperforming all baseline methods.
The execution outcomes match Table I per-category results: background 47.08%, building 59.16%, road 56.74%, water 81.37%, barren 18.15%, forest 46.61%, agriculture 64.31%.
The execution outcomes confirm that UNetMamba achieves best mIoU in 6 out of 7 categories on the LoveDA dataset.
The execution outcomes match Table II: UNetMamba achieves 83.47% mIoU, 90.95% mF1, and 92.51% OA on the ISPRS Vaihingen dataset.
The execution outcomes match Table II efficiency metrics: UNetMamba has 14.76M parameters, 225.71MB memory footprint, and 100.52G FLOPs.
The execution outcomes confirm UNetMamba improves mIoU by 0.87% on LoveDA over the second-best method (UNetFormer at 52.48%).
The execution outcomes confirm UNetMamba improves mIoU by 0.39% on Vaihingen over the second-best methods (DC-Swin, E-PyramidMamba, RS3Mamba all at 83.08%).
The ablation study outcomes match Table III: the baseline model achieves 42.14M params, 183.64G FLOPs, 52.64% mIoU on LoveDA and 83.23% on Vaihingen.
The ablation study outcomes match Table III: MSD-only achieves 13.89M params, 100.52G FLOPs, 52.61% mIoU on LoveDA and 83.18% on Vaihingen.
The ablation study outcomes match Table III: MSD+LSM (full UNetMamba) achieves 14.76M params, 100.52G FLOPs, 53.35% mIoU on LoveDA and 83.47% on Vaihingen.
The ablation results confirm that adding LSM increases mIoU by 0.74% on LoveDA and 0.29% on Vaihingen with only 0.87M additional parameters and no added FLOPs.
The ablation results confirm that replacing the original decoder with MSD reduces parameter count from 42.14M to 13.89M while maintaining competitive mIoU.
